reforms.description: The 1995 Budget proposal includes new initiatives aimed at improving the quality of existing support programmes, focusing on rigorous cost-benefit analysis and targeting subsidies on high social return projects, particularly in research for small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the effectiveness of public funding for research can lead to increased innovation and productivity in small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal was enacted as part of the 1995 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in how public support is evaluated and targeted, which can enhance the impact of funding on innovation.
reforms.source_quote: New initiatives announced in the 1995 Budget proposal focus on improving the quality of existing support programmes rather than cutting overall subsidy levels.
